title: Lesson 429
transcript: Super quick little clarification on a bit of terminology here. We just talked about industrial control systems, ICSs. You might also come across the term OT on the exam, operational technology. What's the difference? Operational technology is a broader term that refers to any hardware software technology used to monitor and control physical processes, devices, including industrial control systems. So operational technology is a broader term contained within operational technology is industrial control systems. So ICS is a subset, is part of OT. And industrial control systems obviously specifically focus on the systems that are used to control industrial processes, water treatment plants, robots in a manufacturing facility. pumps and valves in a nuclear power plant. So just remember OT is the broader term and ICS is a subset of that.
